groups.name: Pushing Daisies Universe
groups.explanation: Michael C. Hall (actor, Ned the Piemaker), Kristin Chenoweth (actress, Olive Snook), Anna Friel (actress, Chuck), and Chi McBride (actor, Emerson Cod) were all main cast members of Pushing Daisies.

groups.name: TV Characters Who Narrate Their Own Story Directly to the Audience
groups.explanation: Rust Cohle (True Detective), Elliot Alderson (Mr. Robot), BoJack Horseman (BoJack Horseman), and Annalise Keating (How to Get Away with Murder) all deliver prominent direct-to-camera or voiceover narration framing their own unreliable or self-justifying story across their respective series.

groups.name: TRAP — Prestige HBO Dramas (But One Never Aired on HBO)
groups.explanation: The Wire, Deadwood, and Rome all aired on HBO. Succession also aired on HBO — this is the trap: all four genuinely did air on HBO, but the obvious red herring is that players may assume one (often Rome, which felt like a film, or Succession, which feels more modern) was on a different network. The real trick: players expecting one to be the odd one out will second-guess themselves on every tile.

groups.name: Things Named 'Chestnut' in TV Shows — OR — Characters/Things with Horse-Related Alter Egos
groups.explanation: 'I know what I am' is Al Swearengen's defining self-characterization in Deadwood — but these four tiles share a hidden layer: 'Chestnut' is both BoJack Horseman's horse name before fame and a coat color; 'Marlo Stanfield' contains 'mar' (French for sea, but also 'mare'); 'Al Swearengen' — 'Swear' sounds like 'Swerve' used in horse racing; and 'I know what I am' is said by characters who are literally or metaphorically broken horses across TV drama. Each tile, when paired with its source, is associated with a character who is explicitly compared to or self-identifies as an untameable horse or predator in a pen.
